research_series.series_name: Bone and Mineral Metabolism Variation Tree

core_interpretation: Bone/mineral tree is strong for spaceflight unloading: BMD/QCT plus CTX/NTX/P1NP and urine calcium provide a high-value musculoskeletal-risk layer. Vitamin D/calcium markers remain context-dependent.
guardrails: Do not infer fracture risk from vitamin D alone.
Do not infer bone loss from one bone turnover marker alone.
Do not infer osteoporosis from microgravity-associated reversible changes without context.
Do not infer renal stone risk from urinary calcium alone.
Do not compare BMD across devices/sites without protocol context.
research_gaps: Limited astronaut data on long-term postflight recovery rates and functional outcomes.
Sparse integration of muscle strength/balance/fall risk with bone outcomes in unloading studies.
Few studies directly linking countermeasure adherence to both biomarker and clinical endpoints.
